visits.note: Initial presentation with fatigue, weight loss, and polyuria. Physical exam reveals a BP of 140/90 and a BMI of 30. The patient has a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. The plan is to start her on a statin and an ACE inhibitor.

Follow-up visit 6 months after initial presentation. The patient reports improved fatigue but persistent polyuria. Her BP is now 130/80 and her HbA1c has decreased to 7.8. However, her LDL has increased to 180. The plan is to increase her statin dose and refer her to a dietician.

Visit 1 year after initial presentation. The patient reports worsening polyuria and noturia. Her BP is now 125/75 and her HbA1c has increased to 8.2. However, her eGFR has decreased to 60. The plan is to refer her to a nephrologist and consider renal-protective medications.

Follow-up visit with nephrologist. The patient reports worsening edema and shortness of breath. Her BP is now 120/70 and her HbA1c has decreased to 7.5. However, her eGFR has decreased further to 45. The plan is to start her on a diuretic and consider dialysis.

Follow-up visit with dialysis team. The patient is now on in-center hemodialysis 3 times a week. Her BP is now 110/60 and her HbA1c has decreased to 6.8. However, she is experiencing some dialysis-induced complications such as muscle cramps and fatigue. The plan is to adjust her medications and fluid balance.
medications: Calcium channel blocker for dialysis-induced muscle cramps
